questions.stem: Under ASC 958, not-for-profit entities classify net assets into which two categories?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Unrestricted and restricted

choices.label: B
choices.text: Without donor restrictions and with donor restrictions

choices.label: C
choices.text: Temporarily restricted, permanently restricted, and unrestricted

choices.label: D
choices.text: Operating and nonoperating

questions.explanation: ASU 2016-14 (codified in ASC 958-205-45-1) simplified net asset classification from three categories to two: net assets without donor restrictions and net assets with donor restrictions. The previous categories of temporarily restricted and permanently restricted were eliminated, though qualitative disclosures about the nature of restrictions are still required.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-14により、純資産の分類が3区分から2区分に簡素化された：Without donor restrictions（制限なし）とWith donor restrictions（制限付き）。従来のtemporarily/permanently restrictedは廃止されたが、制限の性質に関する定性的開示は引き続き必要である。

questions.stem: A donor pledges $500,000 to a not-for-profit, payable in equal installments over 5 years, with no conditions attached. When should the NFP recognize revenue?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As cash is received each year ($100,000 per year)

choices.label: B
choices.text: The full $500,000 at the time of the unconditional pledge, at present value

choices.label: C
choices.text: At the end of 5 years when all payments are received

choices.label: D
choices.text: Only when the donor signs the pledge agreement

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-605-25-2, unconditional promises to give (pledges) are recognized as revenue in the period received at fair value. Multi-year pledges are recorded at present value using an appropriate discount rate. The discount is amortized as contribution revenue over the pledge period. The contribution is classified as with donor restrictions (time restriction).
questions.explanationJa: 無条件のプレッジ（寄付の約束）は受領した期間に公正価値で収益認識する。複数年プレッジは適切な割引率で現在価値に割引して記録する。割引額はプレッジ期間にわたり追加の寄付収益として償却する。この寄付はドナー制限付き（時間制限）に分類される。

questions.stem: A foundation promises $200,000 to a university if the university raises matching funds of $200,000 within one year. This is an example of a:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Unconditional promise to give

choices.label: B
choices.text: Conditional promise to give

choices.label: C
choices.text: Exchange transaction

choices.label: D
choices.text: Agency transaction

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-605-25-5A, a contribution is conditional if it includes both: (1) a barrier that must be overcome (matching requirement) and (2) a right of return of assets or release from obligation if the barrier is not met. A matching fund requirement constitutes a measurable performance-related barrier. Conditional promises are not recognized until the conditions are substantially met.
questions.explanationJa: 寄付が条件付き（conditional）となるには2要件を満たす必要がある：(1) 克服すべき障壁（barrier）の存在（マッチング要件等）、(2) 障壁未充足時の資産返還権または義務免除権。条件付きプレッジは条件が実質的に充足されるまで収益認識しない。

questions.stem: Under ASC 958, contributed services are recognized as revenue only when they:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Are provided by professionals

choices.label: B
choices.text: Create or enhance nonfinancial assets, or require specialized skills that the NFP would otherwise need to purchase

choices.label: C
choices.text: Exceed $5,000 in value

choices.label: D
choices.text: Are approved by the board of directors

questions.explanation: ASC 958-605-25-16 provides two criteria for recognizing contributed services: (a) they create or enhance nonfinancial assets, or (b) they require specialized skills, are provided by individuals possessing those skills, and would typically need to be purchased if not provided by donation. General volunteer services that don't meet these criteria are not recognized.
questions.explanationJa: 寄贈サービスの認識基準は2つ：(a) 非金融資産を創出・増価する、または(b) 専門的スキルを要し、そのスキル保有者が提供し、寄贈がなければ購入が必要なサービス。一般的なボランティア労働はこの基準を満たさず認識しない。

questions.stem: Under ASU 2016-14, how must a not-for-profit present its expenses?

choices.label: A
choices.text: By natural classification only (salaries, rent, utilities)

choices.label: B
choices.text: By functional classification only (program, management, fundraising)

choices.label: C
choices.text: By both natural and functional classification, either on the face of the statements or in the notes

choices.label: D
choices.text: In whatever format management prefers

questions.explanation: ASC 958-720-45-2A (from ASU 2016-14) requires all NFPs to report expenses by both their functional classification (program services, management and general, fundraising) and their natural classification (salaries, depreciation, etc.). This can be presented in a matrix format on the statement of activities, in a separate statement, or in notes.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-14により、全てのNFPは費用を機能別（program services / management & general / fundraising）と性質別（salaries / depreciation等）の両方で報告する義務がある。表示方法は活動計算書のマトリックス形式、独立計算書、または注記のいずれでもよい。

questions.stem: A donor gives $1,000,000 to a university endowment with the stipulation that the principal must be maintained in perpetuity and only the investment income may be spent on scholarships. How is this classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Net assets without donor restrictions

choices.label: B
choices.text: Net assets with donor restrictions (both purpose and perpetual)

choices.label: C
choices.text: A liability until income is earned

choices.label: D
choices.text: Net assets with donor restrictions (perpetual in nature)

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-205-45-1, the $1,000,000 corpus is classified as net assets with donor restrictions because it is subject to a donor-imposed restriction maintained in perpetuity. When investment income is earned, it is initially classified as with donor restrictions (purpose restriction for scholarships) until the restriction is satisfied by awarding scholarships.
questions.explanationJa: $1,000,000の元本はドナーによる永久維持の制限が課されているため、ドナー制限付き純資産（perpetual）に分類する。投資収益はまずドナー制限付き（奨学金目的の制限）に分類され、奨学金授与により制限が充足された時点で制限なしへ振り替える。

questions.stem: Which financial statement is unique to not-for-profit entities (not used by for-profit entities)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Statement of Financial Position

choices.label: B
choices.text: Statement of Activities

choices.label: C
choices.text: Statement of Cash Flows

choices.label: D
choices.text: Statement of Functional Expenses

questions.explanation: The Statement of Functional Expenses is unique to NFPs (required for voluntary health and welfare organizations; optional but common for others under ASU 2016-14 which requires functional expense information). NFPs also use differently named statements (Statement of Financial Position vs. Balance Sheet; Statement of Activities vs. Income Statement) but these serve similar purposes.
questions.explanationJa: Statement of Functional Expenses（機能別費用計算書）はNFP固有の財務諸表である。任意保健福祉団体には必須、その他のNFPでもASU 2016-14により機能別費用情報は必須。NFPは名称の異なる財務諸表を使用するが（Statement of Financial Position / Statement of Activities）、目的は営利企業と同様である。

questions.stem: A donor gives $100,000 to a hospital restricted for cancer research equipment purchases. When the hospital purchases the equipment, what entry is made regarding the restriction?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit Net Assets With Donor Restrictions; Credit Net Assets Without Donor Restrictions (reclassification)

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit Equipment; Credit Cash only

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit Expense; Credit Revenue

choices.label: D
choices.text: No entry regarding restrictions until the equipment is fully depreciated

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-205-45-7, when a donor-imposed restriction is satisfied (the purpose restriction is met by purchasing the equipment), the NFP reclassifies the amount from net assets with donor restrictions to net assets without donor restrictions. This is reported in the statement of activities as 'Net assets released from restrictions.'
questions.explanationJa: ドナー制限が充足された場合（設備購入により目的制限を満たした場合）、制限付き純資産から制限なし純資産へ振り替える。活動計算書では「Net assets released from restrictions」として報告する。

questions.stem: A company pays a not-for-profit $50,000 in exchange for naming rights to a building wing for 10 years. This is classified as:

choices.label: A
choices.text: A contribution without donor restrictions

choices.label: B
choices.text: An exchange transaction (recognized over 10 years)

choices.label: C
choices.text: A contribution with donor restrictions

choices.label: D
choices.text: A conditional contribution

questions.explanation: When the resource provider receives commensurate value in return (naming rights, advertising, etc.), the transaction is an exchange transaction, not a contribution. Under ASC 606, the NFP recognizes revenue over the performance period (10 years) as the naming rights are provided. Contributions are nonreciprocal transfers where the donor does not receive equal value.
questions.explanationJa: 資源提供者が見返りに相応の価値（命名権・広告等）を受け取る場合は交換取引であり、寄付ではない。ASC 606に基づき、命名権の提供期間（10年間）にわたり収益を認識する。寄付は、ドナーが同等の価値を受け取らない非相互的移転である。

questions.stem: Under ASU 2016-14, what qualitative and quantitative information must an NFP disclose regarding liquidity?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Only the current ratio

choices.label: B
choices.text: Quantitative information about financial assets available within one year to meet general expenditures, and qualitative information about how it manages liquidity

choices.label: C
choices.text: A complete five-year cash flow projection

choices.label: D
choices.text: Only total cash and cash equivalents

questions.explanation: ASC 958-210-50-1A (from ASU 2016-14) requires NFPs to disclose both quantitative and qualitative information useful in assessing liquidity. Quantitatively, they must show financial assets available within one year to meet cash needs for general expenditures. Qualitatively, they describe how they manage liquidity and liquidity risk.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-14により、NFPは流動性に関する定量的・定性的情報の両方を開示する。定量的には1年以内に一般支出を賄うために利用可能な金融資産を示し、定性的には流動性およびリスクの管理方法を記述する。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, a museum receives an unconditional pledge of $300,000 payable in three equal annual installments of $100,000 starting December 31, Year 1. The appropriate discount rate is 5%. What amount of contribution revenue with donor restrictions does the museum recognize on January 1, Year 1?
questions.explanation: Multi-year pledges are recorded at present value. PV = $100,000/(1.05)¹ + $100,000/(1.05)² + $100,000/(1.05)³ = $95,238 + $90,703 + $86,384 = $272,325. This is the PV of a 3-year ordinary annuity at 5%. The discount ($27,675) is amortized as additional contribution revenue over the pledge period.

questions.stem: On July 1, Year 1, an NFP receives $240,000 from a donor who stipulates the funds must be used equally over Years 2, 3, and 4 ($80,000 per year for program activities). What amount is reclassified from 'with donor restrictions' to 'without donor restrictions' during Year 2?
questions.explanation: The donor imposed a time restriction: $80,000 to be used in each of Years 2, 3, and 4. In Year 1, the entire $240,000 is recognized as contribution revenue with donor restrictions. As each year's restriction is satisfied by the passage of time and use of funds, $80,000 is released (reclassified to without donor restrictions). In Year 2, $80,000 is reclassified.
questions.explanationJa: ドナーは時間制限を課した：各Year 2、3、4に$80,000ずつ使用。Year 1に$240,000全額を制限付き寄付収益として認識する。各年の制限が時間経過と資金使用で充足されるたびに$80,000を制限なし純資産へ振り替える。Year 2の振替額は$80,000。

questions.stem: A university has a donor-restricted endowment fund with an original gift of $5,000,000. Due to investment losses, the fair value of the endowment drops to $4,700,000. Under UPMIFA and ASC 958, how is the $300,000 decline reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a reduction of net assets without donor restrictions

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a reduction of net assets with donor restrictions

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a liability to the donor

choices.label: D
choices.text: No financial statement impact; disclosed in notes only

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-205-45-10 (reflecting UPMIFA), investment losses that reduce the fair value of a donor-restricted endowment below the original gift amount (an 'underwater' endowment) are reported as a reduction of net assets with donor restrictions—not without donor restrictions. Required disclosures include the aggregate amount by which endowments are underwater.
questions.explanationJa: 基本財産（endowment）の公正価値が当初寄付額を下回る場合（underwater endowment）、その投資損失はドナー制限付き純資産の減少として報告する。制限なし純資産ではない。水面下にある合計額の開示が必要である。

questions.stem: A charity receives donated food valued at $180,000 and donated volunteer services (general labor, not specialized) valued at $60,000. What total amount of contribution revenue should be recognized?
questions.explanation: Donated food (a nonfinancial asset) is recognized at fair value ($180,000) as contribution revenue. However, general volunteer labor that does not require specialized skills and does not create or enhance a nonfinancial asset does NOT meet the recognition criteria under ASC 958-605-25-16. Therefore, only $180,000 is recognized.
questions.explanationJa: 寄贈食品（非金融資産）は公正価値$180,000で寄付収益として認識する。一方、専門的スキルを要しない一般的ボランティア労働は認識基準を満たさない。よって認識額は$180,000のみ。

questions.stem: A research institute receives a $1,500,000 government grant with a condition that the institute must incur qualifying research expenditures. During Year 1, the institute incurs $900,000 of qualifying expenditures. What amount of grant revenue should the institute recognize in Year 1?
questions.explanation: This is a conditional contribution with a measurable performance-related barrier (incurring qualifying research expenditures). Under ASC 958-605-25-5A, revenue is recognized as the barrier is overcome. Since $900,000 of qualifying expenditures were incurred, $900,000 of the conditional grant is recognized as revenue. The remaining $600,000 is reported as a refundable advance (liability).
questions.explanationJa: 適格研究支出の発生が障壁（barrier）となる条件付き補助金である。障壁の克服に応じて収益を認識する。適格支出$900,000が発生したため$900,000を収益認識する。残り$600,000は返還可能前受金（refundable advance、負債）として報告する。

questions.stem: A donor establishes a charitable remainder annuity trust with $800,000 in assets. The trust will pay the donor $50,000 annually for 10 years, then the remainder goes to the NFP. The present value of the annuity payments to the donor is $350,000. What amount does the NFP recognize as contribution revenue?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-30, when an NFP is the remainder beneficiary of a charitable remainder trust, it recognizes the assets at fair value ($800,000), a liability for the PV of future payments to the income beneficiary ($350,000), and contribution revenue for the difference. Revenue = $800,000 − $350,000 = $450,000, classified as with donor restrictions.
questions.explanationJa: NFPが残余受益者（remainder beneficiary）の場合、信託資産を公正価値$800,000で認識し、収入受益者への将来支払のPV $350,000を負債とし、差額を寄付収益とする。収益 = $800,000 - $350,000 = $450,000。ドナー制限付きに分類する。

questions.stem: An NFP has total occupancy costs of $600,000. Floor space allocation: Program services 60%, Management and general 25%, Fundraising 15%. What amount of occupancy cost is allocated to program services?
questions.explanation: Joint costs are allocated to functional categories using a reasonable method. Occupancy costs allocated to program services = $600,000 × 60% = $360,000. Management and general = $600,000 × 25% = $150,000. Fundraising = $600,000 × 15% = $90,000. The allocation method must be rational and systematic.

questions.stem: A university's board adopts a spending policy of 4.5% of a 3-year rolling average fair value for its endowment. The 3-year average fair values are: Year 1: $20,000,000, Year 2: $21,000,000, Year 3: $22,000,000. What is the maximum spending appropriation for Year 4?
questions.explanation: 3-year rolling average = ($20,000,000 + $21,000,000 + $22,000,000) / 3 = $63,000,000 / 3 = $21,000,000. Spending appropriation = $21,000,000 × 4.5% = $945,000. Under UPMIFA, the board's spending policy determines how much can be appropriated from the endowment for expenditure.
questions.explanationJa: 3年移動平均 =（$20,000,000 + $21,000,000 + $22,000,000）/ 3 = $21,000,000。支出充当額 = $21,000,000 × 4.5% = $945,000。UPMIFAでは理事会の支出方針（spending policy）が基本財産からの充当可能額を決定する。

questions.stem: A community foundation receives $500,000 from Donor X and agrees to distribute it to Charity Y as directed by Donor X. The foundation has no variance power. How does the community foundation report this transaction?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Contribution revenue of $500,000 with donor restrictions

choices.label: B
choices.text: Increase in assets $500,000 and increase in liabilities $500,000 (no revenue)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Contribution revenue of $500,000 without donor restrictions

choices.label: D
choices.text: No entry until funds are distributed to Charity Y

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-605-25-24, when an NFP receives assets as an intermediary/agent to transfer to a specified beneficiary (and has no variance power to redirect the assets), it does not recognize contribution revenue. Instead, it records a liability to the specified beneficiary. Assets increase $500,000, Liability (due to Charity Y) increases $500,000.
questions.explanationJa: NFPが仲介者/代理人（agent）として資産を受領し、転用権限（variance power）を持たない場合、寄付収益は認識しない。代わりに指定受益者への負債を計上する。資産+$500,000、負債（Charity Y向け）+$500,000。

questions.subtopic: Statement of Activities - Change in Net Assets
questions.stem: An NFP has the following Year 1 activity: Contributions without donor restrictions $2,000,000; Contributions with donor restrictions $800,000; Net assets released from restrictions $500,000; Total expenses $1,900,000. What is the change in net assets without donor restrictions?
questions.explanation: Change in net assets without donor restrictions = Revenue without restrictions + Net assets released from restrictions − Expenses = $2,000,000 + $500,000 − $1,900,000 = $600,000. The $800,000 with donor restrictions affects only the 'with restrictions' column. Released restrictions ($500,000) are added to the 'without restrictions' column.
questions.explanationJa: 制限なし純資産の変動 = 制限なし収入 + 制限解除振替 - 費用 = $2,000,000 + $500,000 - $1,900,000 = $600,000。制限付き寄付$800,000は「制限付き」列にのみ影響する。制限解除$500,000は「制限なし」列に加算される。

questions.stem: An NFP receives $100,000 cash restricted by the donor for the purchase of equipment. At year-end, the cash has not been spent. How should this be reported on the statement of financial position?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Cash of $100,000; Net assets without donor restrictions of $100,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Cash of $100,000; Net assets with donor restrictions of $100,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Restricted cash of $100,000; Deferred revenue of $100,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: No reporting until equipment is purchased

questions.explanation: When an NFP receives cash restricted by the donor for a specific purpose (equipment purchase), it is reported as cash (or separately as restricted cash if the NFP chooses) on the statement of financial position. The corresponding net asset classification is 'with donor restrictions' until the restriction is satisfied (equipment is purchased).
questions.explanationJa: NFPがドナー制限付き現金（設備購入目的）を受領した場合、財政状態計算書で現金（または選択により制限付き現金として区分表示）として報告する。対応する純資産区分は制限が充足（設備購入）されるまで「ドナー制限付き」。

questions.stem: An NFP receives unconditional pledges totaling $500,000, payable over 3 years. Management estimates 5% will be uncollectible. Using a discount rate of 4%, the present value of the pledges is $460,000. At what amount should pledges receivable be reported?
questions.explanation: Multi-year unconditional promises to give are measured at the present value of estimated future cash flows. Net collectible amount = $500,000 × 95% = $475,000. Present value at 4% = $460,000 (as given). However, the uncollectible estimate should also be applied to the discounted amount: $460,000 × 95% = $437,000. Alternatively: PV of expected collections = PV of ($500,000 × 0.95) = $460,000 × 0.95 = $437,000.
questions.explanationJa: 複数年の無条件寄付約束は将来キャッシュフローの現在価値で測定する。回収見込額 = $500,000 × 95% = $475,000。4%での現在価値 = $460,000（提示値）。回収不能見積りも割引後に適用：$460,000 × 95% = $437,000。

questions.stem: A CPA donates 100 hours of accounting services (normally billed at $200/hour) to an NFP to prepare its annual financial statements. The services meet the recognition criteria under ASC 958. What amount of contribution revenue should the NFP recognize?

choices.label: D
choices.text: The amount is disclosed in notes only

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-605-25-16, contributed services are recognized as revenue if they (a) create or enhance nonfinancial assets, or (b) require specialized skills, are provided by individuals possessing those skills, and would typically need to be purchased if not donated. CPA services for financial statement preparation meet these criteria. Revenue = 100 hours × $200 = $20,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 958-605-25-16では、寄附サービスは(a) 非金融資産を創出・増価する、または(b) 専門的技術を要し、その技術を持つ個人が提供し、寄附がなければ通常購入する必要がある場合に収益認識する。CPA による財務諸表作成はこの基準を満たす。収益 = 100時間 × $200 = $20,000。

questions.stem: An NFP professional association charges $500 annual membership dues that entitle members to a monthly journal (fair value $120/year) and conference discounts (fair value $80/year). How should the $500 dues be classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Entirely as contribution revenue

choices.label: B
choices.text: Entirely as exchange revenue

choices.label: C
choices.text: $200 exchange revenue and $300 contribution revenue

choices.label: D
choices.text: $500 exchange revenue with no contribution element

questions.explanation: When membership dues provide both an exchange element (member benefits with determinable value) and a contribution element (excess payment over fair value of benefits), the transaction is bifurcated. Exchange portion = FV of journal ($120) + FV of discounts ($80) = $200. Contribution portion = $500 − $200 = $300.
questions.explanationJa: 会費がメンバー特典（交換要素）と寄附要素を含む場合、取引を二分する。交換部分 = ジャーナルFV（$120）+ 割引FV（$80）= $200。寄附部分 = $500 − $200 = $300。

questions.stem: A not-for-profit hospital provides services with gross charges of $2,000,000. Contractual adjustments with insurance companies total $600,000, and charity care provided at cost is $100,000. What is the net patient service revenue reported?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 954, net patient service revenue = Gross patient charges − Contractual adjustments. Charity care is not reported as revenue or as an expense deduction — it is disclosed in the notes. Therefore: $2,000,000 − $600,000 = $1,400,000. Charity care ($100,000 at cost) is reported only in note disclosures.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 954では、純患者サービス収入 = 総患者請求額 − 契約調整額。慈善医療は収入にも費用控除にも含めず注記で開示する。したがって$2,000,000 − $600,000 = $1,400,000。慈善医療（原価$100,000）は注記のみで報告。

questions.stem: An NFP's board of directors designates $200,000 of unrestricted net assets for a building renovation fund. How should this designation be reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As net assets with donor restrictions

choices.label: B
choices.text: As net assets without donor restrictions, with disclosure of the board designation

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a separate category between restricted and unrestricted

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a liability for the committed renovation

questions.explanation: Board-designated net assets remain classified as 'without donor restrictions' because the board can reverse the designation at any time. Board designations are internal, not externally imposed. They may be disclosed on the face of the financial statements or in the notes, but they do not create a separate net asset class.
questions.explanationJa: 理事会指定純資産は、理事会がいつでも指定を解除できるため「ドナー制限なし」に分類される。理事会の指定は内部的なもので外部制約ではない。財務諸表本文または注記で開示できるが、別個の純資産区分にはならない。

questions.stem: NFP A receives $300,000 from a donor with instructions to transfer it to NFP B, a specified beneficiary. NFP A has variance power to redirect the funds. How should NFP A report this transaction?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a liability of $300,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: As contribution revenue of $300,000 with donor restrictions

choices.label: C
choices.text: As contribution revenue of $300,000 without donor restrictions

choices.label: D
choices.text: No entry until the funds are transferred to NFP B

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-605-25-24, when an NFP intermediary has variance power (the ability to redirect assets to another beneficiary), it recognizes contribution revenue rather than a liability. Since the donor specified NFP B as the beneficiary, the contribution is reported with donor restrictions until NFP A exercises its variance power or transfers the funds.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 958-605-25-24に基づき、NFP仲介者が転用権限（variance power）を有する場合、負債ではなく寄附収益を認識する。ドナーがNFP Bを受益者に指定しているため、NFP Aが転用権限を行使するか資金を移転するまでドナー制限付きの寄附として報告する。

questions.stem: A church allows an NFP to use its meeting hall rent-free for one year. The fair rental value is $36,000. Under what condition should the NFP recognize this as a contribution?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Always recognize as contribution revenue

choices.label: B
choices.text: Only if the use creates or enhances nonfinancial assets

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only if an unconditional promise to give exists

choices.label: D
choices.text: Never — in-kind use of facilities cannot be recognized

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-605-25-2, contributions of use of assets (such as facilities) are unconditional transfers and should be recognized as contribution revenue at fair value when received. Unlike contributed services (which have specific recognition criteria), contributed use of long-lived assets (facilities, equipment) is always recognized when it constitutes an unconditional gift.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 958-605-25-2に基づき、資産の使用権の寄附（施設等）は無条件移転であり、受領時に公正価値で寄附収益として認識する。特定の認識基準がある寄附サービスとは異なり、長期性資産（施設、設備）の使用権の寄附は無条件の贈与であれば常に認識する。

questions.stem: Under ASU 2016-14, an NFP must present expenses by which classification(s) in its financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Natural classification only

choices.label: B
choices.text: Functional classification only

choices.label: C
choices.text: Both natural and functional classification

choices.label: D
choices.text: Either natural or functional, at the entity's election

questions.explanation: ASU 2016-14 requires all NFPs to present expenses by both functional classification (program services, management and general, fundraising) and natural classification (salaries, rent, depreciation, etc.) in one location. This can be on the face of the statement of activities, in a separate statement, or in the notes.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-14は全てのNFPに対し、費用を機能別分類（事業サービス、管理一般、資金調達）と性質別分類（給与、賃料、減価償却等）の両方で1箇所にまとめて表示することを要求する。活動計算書本文、別個の計算書、または注記で表示可能。

questions.stem: An NFP has an endowment with a $1,000,000 corpus restricted in perpetuity. Investment income of $50,000 is earned; the donor stipulates that investment income must be used for scholarships. How should the $50,000 be classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Net assets without donor restrictions

choices.label: B
choices.text: Net assets with donor restrictions

choices.label: C
choices.text: Temporarily restricted until spent on scholarships, then reclassified

choices.label: D
choices.text: Both B and C are correct

questions.explanation: Since the donor restricted investment income to scholarships, the $50,000 is initially classified as net assets with donor restrictions (purpose restriction). When the funds are actually spent on scholarships, the restriction is met and the amount is reclassified to 'without donor restrictions' via net assets released from restrictions. Under ASU 2016-14, 'temporarily restricted' is now simply 'with donor restrictions.'
questions.explanationJa: ドナーが運用益を奨学金に制限したため、$50,000は当初「ドナー制限付き純資産」（目的制限）に分類される。奨学金に実際に使用された時点で制限充足となり、制限解除振替で「制限なし」に再分類される。ASU 2016-14では「一時制限付き」は「ドナー制限付き」に統合された。

questions.stem: A museum purchases a painting for $50,000 to add to its permanent collection. The museum's policy is to not capitalize collection items. What is the proper accounting treatment?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Capitalize at $50,000 and depreciate over its useful life

choices.label: B
choices.text: Capitalize at $50,000 with no depreciation

choices.label: C
choices.text: Do not capitalize; reduce net assets without donor restrictions by $50,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Capitalize at fair value, which may differ from cost

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-360-25, an NFP may elect either to capitalize or not capitalize its collection items, provided the collection meets certain criteria (held for public exhibition, protected, subject to a policy requiring proceeds from sales to be used for new acquisitions). If the policy is not to capitalize, the purchase reduces net assets. The entity must apply this policy consistently to all collections.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 958-360-25に基づき、NFPはコレクション品目を資産計上するか否かを選択できる（公開展示、保護、売却代金を新規取得に充当する方針が条件）。資産計上しない方針の場合、購入は純資産を減少させる。この方針は全コレクションに一貫して適用する必要がある。

questions.stem: Under ASU 2016-14, how should an NFP report its fundraising expenses?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Included with program expenses on the statement of activities

choices.label: B
choices.text: Reported as a separate functional category distinct from program services and management

choices.label: C
choices.text: Disclosed only in the notes to financial statements

choices.label: D
choices.text: Netted against contribution revenue

questions.explanation: NFPs must report expenses by functional classification: program services, management and general, and fundraising. Fundraising is a required separate functional category and cannot be combined with program expenses or management and general expenses. This requirement provides transparency about the cost of raising funds.
questions.explanationJa: NFPは費用を機能別に報告する：事業サービス、管理一般、資金調達。資金調達は独立した必須の機能区分であり、事業費用や管理一般費用と合算できない。この要件は資金調達コストの透明性を確保する。

questions.stem: An NFP mails a direct-mail piece that includes both a fundraising appeal and educational content about its mission. Under ASC 958-720, which criteria must be met to allocate joint costs between fundraising and program functions?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The fundraising component must generate more than 50% of the costs

choices.label: B
choices.text: Purpose, audience, and content criteria must all be met

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only the purpose criterion must be met

choices.label: D
choices.text: Joint cost allocation is always prohibited

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-720-45-28, joint costs of activities that include a fundraising appeal may be allocated between fundraising and program (or management) functions only if all three criteria are met: (1) purpose — a program or management function would be conducted even without the fundraising component; (2) audience — the audience is selected based on its need for the program message; (3) content — the activity calls for a specific action beyond making a contribution.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 958-720-45-28に基づき、資金調達アピールを含む活動の共同費用は、3つの基準全てを満たす場合のみ資金調達と事業（または管理）機能に配分できる：(1) 目的 — 資金調達要素がなくても事業・管理機能が実施される、(2) 対象者 — プログラムメッセージの必要性に基づき選定、(3) 内容 — 寄付以外の具体的な行動を要請。

questions.stem: An NFP receives a 3-year unconditional pledge of $30,000 per year. The risk-adjusted discount rate is 5%. What is the present value of the pledge at inception?
questions.explanation: Multi-year unconditional promises to give are recorded at the present value of future cash flows. PV = $30,000 / 1.05 + $30,000 / 1.05² + $30,000 / 1.05³ = $28,571 + $27,211 + $25,915 = $81,697. The discount is amortized as contribution revenue over the pledge period.
questions.explanationJa: 複数年の無条件寄付約束は将来キャッシュフローの現在価値で記録する。PV = $30,000/1.05 + $30,000/1.05² + $30,000/1.05³ = $28,571 + $27,211 + $25,915 = $81,697。割引額は寄付期間にわたり寄附収益として償却する。

questions.stem: An NFP receives 500 boxes of canned food with a fair value of $8,000 from a food manufacturer. The food will be distributed to program beneficiaries. How should this be recorded?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No entry; in-kind contributions of goods are not recognized

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit Inventory $8,000; Credit Contribution Revenue — with donor restrictions $8,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit Inventory $8,000; Credit Contribution Revenue — without donor restrictions $8,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Disclose in notes only at the manufacturer's cost

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-605-25-2, unconditional contributions of nonfinancial assets (goods) are recognized as contribution revenue at fair value when received. Since the donor did not impose explicit restrictions on how the food is used, it is classified as without donor restrictions. The fact that the NFP intends to use it in its programs is an internal designation, not a donor restriction.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 958-605-25-2に基づき、非金融資産（物品）の無条件寄附は受領時に公正価値で寄附収益として認識する。ドナーが食品の使途に明示的な制限を課していないため、ドナー制限なしに分類。NFPが事業で使用する意図は内部的な指定であり、ドナー制限ではない。

questions.stem: Two NFPs decide to merge. NFP X has net assets of $2,000,000 and NFP Y has net assets of $800,000. Under ASC 958-805, how is a merger (as opposed to an acquisition) accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The purchase method with goodwill recognition

choices.label: B
choices.text: The carryover method, combining assets and liabilities at carrying values

choices.label: C
choices.text: The acquisition method with fair value measurement

choices.label: D
choices.text: The pooling of interests method

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-805, NFP mergers (where no single entity is the acquirer and the combination is not an exchange transaction) are accounted for using the carryover method. Assets and liabilities of the merging entities are combined at their carrying values. Net assets are combined and reclassified as appropriate. No goodwill is recognized in a merger.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 958-805では、NFPの合併（単一の取得者が存在せず交換取引でない場合）は帳簿価額引継法（carryover method）で処理する。合併各団体の資産・負債を帳簿価額で合算し、純資産を適切に再分類する。合併ではのれんは認識しない。

questions.stem: An NFP holds a gala dinner. Tickets are sold for $500 each; the fair value of the dinner is $150 per person. 200 tickets are sold. How should the revenue be reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Special event revenue of $100,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Contribution revenue of $70,000 and special event revenue of $30,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Contribution revenue of $100,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Special event revenue of $30,000 only

questions.explanation: Total proceeds = 200 × $500 = $100,000. The exchange element (fair value of dinner) = 200 × $150 = $30,000 is reported as special event revenue. The contribution element (excess over fair value) = $100,000 − $30,000 = $70,000 is reported as contribution revenue without donor restrictions.
questions.explanationJa: 総収入 = 200 × $500 = $100,000。交換要素（夕食のFV）= 200 × $150 = $30,000はスペシャルイベント収入として報告。寄附要素（FV超過額）= $100,000 − $30,000 = $70,000はドナー制限なし寄附収益として報告。

questions.stem: Under ASU 2016-14, which of the following statements about the 'measure of operations' for NFPs is correct?

choices.label: A
choices.text: All NFPs must present a measure of operations on the statement of activities

choices.label: B
choices.text: If an NFP elects to present a measure of operations, it must include all changes in net assets without donor restrictions

choices.label: C
choices.text: If presented, the operating measure must be an intermediate subtotal within the without-restrictions column

choices.label: D
choices.text: The measure of operations must exclude investment returns

questions.explanation: Under ASU 2016-14, presenting a measure of operations (operating income) is optional, not required. If an NFP chooses to present one, it must be an intermediate subtotal within the changes in net assets without donor restrictions. The NFP has flexibility in defining what is 'operating' but must describe its policy in the notes.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-14では、経常活動の指標（営業利益）の表示は任意であり必須ではない。表示する場合、ドナー制限なし純資産の変動の中間小計としなければならない。何を「経常的」とするかはNFPに裁量があるが、その方針を注記で説明する必要がある。

questions.stem: An NFP receives $200,000 from a foundation. The grant agreement stipulates that the funds must be returned if the NFP does not match the grant with $100,000 from other sources by year-end. The NFP has not yet raised the matching funds. How should the $200,000 be reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Contribution revenue — with donor restrictions

choices.label: B
choices.text: Contribution revenue — without donor restrictions

choices.label: C
choices.text: Refundable advance (liability)

choices.label: D
choices.text: Deferred revenue

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-605-25-5A, a conditional contribution is one that includes a barrier that must be overcome and a right of return or release. The matching requirement ($100,000 from other sources) is a barrier. Until the barrier is met, the recipient reports the cash received as a refundable advance (liability), not as revenue.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 958-605-25-5Aに基づき、条件付き寄附は克服すべき障壁と返還権または免除権を含む。マッチング要件（他の資金源から$100,000）は障壁である。障壁が克服されるまで、受領した現金は返還予定前受金（負債）として報告し、収益としない。

questions.stem: A state has adopted UPMIFA (Uniform Prudent Management of Institutional Funds Act). An NFP endowment has an original gift of $1,000,000 and current fair value of $1,200,000. Under the total return approach, which amount may the NFP appropriate for spending?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Only the $200,000 in excess of the original gift

choices.label: B
choices.text: An amount determined prudently considering the endowment's purposes, duration, and economic conditions — potentially from both income and appreciation

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only realized gains, not unrealized gains

choices.label: D
choices.text: Nothing — endowment assets may never be spent

questions.explanation: Under UPMIFA (adopted by ASC 958-205), the total return approach allows an NFP to appropriate endowment funds for expenditure based on prudent consideration of factors including the purpose and duration of the endowment fund, general economic conditions, and possible effects of inflation. The board may spend from both income and appreciation (realized and unrealized), not limited to the amount above the original gift.
questions.explanationJa: UPMIFA（ASC 958-205が採用）に基づくトータルリターン・アプローチでは、NFPは基金の目的・存続期間、一般経済状況、インフレの影響等を慎重に考慮してエンダウメント資金を支出に充当できる。理事会は収入と評価益（実現・未実現）の両方から支出でき、元本超過額のみに限定されない。
